reviews.review_description: Waiting for updates but for now you get to pay 15€ for an early access game offering just 2 sceneries with only one being available during a different moment of the day (sunset). You can choose among free, time or multiplayer paddle. Graphics and physics are basic.

reviews.review_title: Needs a little work, But fun
reviews.review_description: As a real life kayaker, the motions feel pretty close, I wish the kayaked rocked a little to make it much more realistic. Definitely needs a lot of sea life added. Have only come across a few fish and a giant octopus. The ice shows through the kayak, and a few spots where you can paddle into land. Overall nice start to something. Hoping it gets a lot of updates, it's promising.

The physics of the Kayak motion and operation is very good, which is a great start. This release seems to be very early access. At this time, I feel it has great potential to be a great form of fun, relaxation and very competitive. 
I am hoping that the developer will put the time and work into this. It seems that there hasn’t been much new content lately.
Aside from adding more locations, like some of the locations already in their Sailing app, it would be great to add possibly a white water river or stream to travel on. Also an improvement to the graphics would make it more interesting and immersive. 
I hope that the developer will respond and provide a bit more information as to what content and when an update or updates will be announced.
Keep up the good work and this will be a great game.
Wanted a rowing exercise game and took a chance with this.  Glad I did!!  There are racing sections against bots and I lowered the height of the kayak in order to squat when rowing!  Nice workout!

Needs enhancement ASAP

Who’s the joker who glued this paddle to my hands?

Nothing to do or see here.

Starting with the kayak, nice clear graphics with well thought out indicators for speed ect. Paddle works well with good haptic feedback. Water and reflections are very impressive too. 
Images of the islands and structures just need more attention to detail which the Quest 3 is more than capable of even as a standalone headset.
The game is a bit boring . I have played 2 times and didn’t want to log in again 😑

Good Kayak game. I was quite chill while paddling around a cozy island with an amazing sunrise. 5 stars ★

PS: For perfect detail, please add some moving fish and plants to the seafloor. That would be awesome.
I was able to sail right through some on shore structures; also the water effects are not that realistic. Also makes me a bit dizzy.

Wanna get away from it all? Wanna kayak in the arctic or enjoy the ocean during the sunset? This is your game. For those who enjoy listening to the breeze (and if you're lucky enough to have a head strap that blows with a fan) the experience is just like the real thing.....without the splash zone. Fun and relaxing game. Can even do time trials if you wanna get a workout or test yourself.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your positive feedback! ❤️

We're thrilled to hear that you're enjoying the serene and immersive experience of MarineVerse Kayaking. We're continuously working on adding more stunning locations and features to enhance your virtual kayaking adventures. 

Happy paddling! 🌅

reviews.review_description:
I am one of the early testers of the original MarineVerse Cup game, so I have been aware of this developers work for many years now.

When I realized there was a kayaking game developed by the same person I immediately got it today. 

Before today, I only knew of and own Kayak VR: Mirage on PCVR. Which anyone who has it or has tried it can agree it is mindblowing in its graphics and physics.

I knew going into getting MarineVerse Kayaking that the graphics would not be equal to anything PCVR can produce, and that’s ok. It’s not expected.

Physics, however, I did have an expectation to the similarities of Kayak VR: Mirage…

It’s on the right path, but it needs work. 

The issue really stood out when I tried time trials. A lot of frustrating zig-zagging when turning in order to keep your line straight in the direction you want to line up to get the “stars”. 

I feel the reason for this is because when you dip your oar into the water to turn (no matter if you dip it deep or just lightly under the water), it turns “sharply” in the direction you want to go as opposed to the realism of being in the water and having smooth realistic experience depending on your “dipping” depth. 

It was frustrating. Turning has to be smooth to provide physics realism.

I am very aware this is early access right now and it’s not the “final” game product, but I highly advise the developer to jump into Kayak VR: Mirage and learn from that game in order to get the turning physics dialed in better. 

And I know it’s not a Quest processor limitation, this is definitely something that can be tweaked and fixed to make the kayaking motion/turning physics dialed in better. 

If I see an improvement on this, a 5 star updated review will be guaranteed.

I look forward to the improvements!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your detailed and thoughtful feedback. 

We're glad to hear that you're familiar with our work and excited about MarineVerse Kayaking. 

Your insights on the turning mechanics are valuable, and we'll definitely look into refining the physics to provide a smoother and more realistic experience. We appreciate your support and patience as we continue to develop and improve the app.

Stay tuned for updates, and we hope to earn that 5-star review from you soon!

Greg

reviews.review_description: Great game with lots of potential.  I would like to see lots of fish small and large around the kayak and as I go towards the shore and some more activity to look at in the sky, .  I say chilling and relaxing it isn't when you go to race!!  My arms felt it afterwards....  Hope the Devs continue to work on this game......  Visuals are great, the physics are great.. Good fun all around..... Just need more wildlife....

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback Magicmerlin007!

Agree, having more wildlife will make the exploration more fun.

Happy to hear that racing offered a good workout :)

reviews.review_description: Not a bad game. Keeps my 11 year old active 😊 and gives him a bit of variety

Even though it's in its early development this app is already a great fun fitness and or chill with friends experience.  The physics are good enough to have good control over the boat and the environments are big enough for exploring. Moreover its the only multiplayer canoeing app out there.

developer_response.body: Hey Tartanstorm, thank you for supporting us on this journey to develop a great standalone kayaking app!

reviews.review_description: This app is such a wonderful escape! The setting is absolutely gorgeous, and so peaceful. I love how clear the water is and how realistic it feels. It is amazing how the water seems to get deeper as you paddle away from the shore! This will be so encouraging during the Winter months!

developer_response.body:
We are so happy to hear about your experience with MarineVerse Kayaking SailingMurphy!

If you haven't tried it yet, make sure to check out the new Arctic level.

reviews.review_description: Love the physics and the relaxing time that the game gives you

Kayaking is a very popular sport currently. I would love to see a few things change in this game if possible. 
1) It's very lonely doing Time Trials/Freeplay. Can we have some birds and fish/crabs in the water? I've only played a couple of Time Trials but it would make all the difference.
2) Some weather effects, waves/rain etc?
3) Some river kayaking (white knuckle) would be good too as the sea is so big and open.
The graphics are nice and it is quite easy to get to grips with, I am not sure if I am meant to stand or sit when playing it.

Also perhaps a calorie summary at the end of the session with distance, number of strokes (if thats what its called).
Good graphics, nice environment and cloud details. hand tracking works fine. but most of the time it will end up in the menu. has to be fixed. keep up the good work. expecting more updates in future.

I know that this app is still developing, however, based on what I've seen, you're on the right track with this.
I can clearly see something very exciting brewing from this app in the upcoming months.
I like the stunning water effects and the ocean sounds are so tranquil..  love   the realistic vibrations as you paddle (that's genius..).  The sunset & daytime environments look GREAT..

I hope you can add rainy environments, jungle environments, iceland environments and.. mountain scenery environments.
Would love to see fish and more locations added.
